description: Travel narratives extracted from Genesis — movements of persons or groups between named locations

journeys.description: Expulsion from the Garden of Eden to the east

waypoints.event: sent out from the Garden of Eden
driven east of Eden

journeys.description: Cain goes out from the presence of the LORD to the land of Nod, east of Eden

waypoints.event: departed from the presence of the LORD
settled in the land of Nod, east of Eden

journeys.description: People migrate to the plain of Shinar and build the Tower of Babel

waypoints.event: found a plain in the land of Shinar and settled there
LORD confused languages at Babel; people scattered

journeys.description: Terah takes Abraham, Sarai, and Lot from Ur of the Chaldeans toward Canaan, but settles in Haran

waypoints.event: departed from Ur of the Chaldeans
settled in Haran instead of continuing to Canaan

journeys.description: Abraham's initial journey from Haran through Canaan: Shechem, Bethel, and on to the Negev

waypoints.event: departed from Haran at age 75
arrived in the land of Canaan
passed through to the place of Shechem, the oak of Moreh
moved to the hill country, pitched tent between Bethel and Ai
journeyed on toward the Negev

journeys.description: Abraham goes down to Egypt because of famine, then returns to the Negev and back to Bethel

waypoints.event: in the Negev when famine struck
went down to Egypt to sojourn because of famine
went up from Egypt into the Negev
journeyed from the Negev back to Bethel, to the place of his earlier altar

journeys.description: Lot separates from Abraham, choosing the Jordan plain and settling near Sodom

waypoints.event: at Bethel with Abraham before the separation
chose the plain of the Jordan and journeyed east
pitched his tent as far as Sodom

journeys.description: After Lot's departure, Abraham moves to the oaks of Mamre at Hebron

waypoints.event: at Bethel after separation from Lot
moved tent and dwelt by the oaks of Mamre at Hebron

journeys.description: Campaign of the four eastern kings through Transjordan and the Negev before the battle at the Valley of Siddim

waypoints.event: defeated the Emim at Shaveh-Kiriathaim
defeated the Horites in their mountain Seir
advanced as far as El-Paran by the wilderness
turned back to En-Mishpat (Kadesh)
struck the Amorites at Hazazon-Tamar
joined battle in the Valley of Siddim

journeys.description: Abraham pursues the four kings from Mamre to Dan and beyond Damascus to rescue Lot, then returns via Salem

waypoints.event: received news of Lot's capture at the oaks of Mamre
pursued the kings as far as Dan
defeated and pursued them to Hobah, north of Damascus
met king of Sodom at the Valley of Shaveh (King's Valley)
met Melchizedek king of Salem who brought bread and wine

journeys.description: Hagar flees from Sarai into the wilderness toward Shur, found by the angel at Beer-lahai-roi

waypoints.event: fled from Sarai (Abraham dwelling in Canaan near Mamre)
found by the angel on the road to Shur
named the well Beer-lahai-roi, between Kadesh and Bered

journeys.description: Lot flees from Sodom's destruction to Zoar, then up into the mountains

waypoints.event: angels arrived at Sodom, Lot sitting in the gate
fled to Zoar as the sun rose
afraid to stay in Zoar, went up into the mountains with his daughters

journeys.description: Abraham journeys from Mamre/Hebron area toward the Negev and sojourns in Gerar

waypoints.event: dwelling by the oaks of Mamre
journeyed toward the Negev, between Kadesh and Shur
sojourned in Gerar

journeys.description: Abraham settles at Beer-sheba after making a covenant with Abimelech

waypoints.event: sojourning in Gerar
made a covenant at Beer-sheba with Abimelech
planted a tamarisk tree and settled at Beer-sheba

journeys.description: Hagar and Ishmael are expelled, wander in the wilderness of Beer-sheba, and settle in Paran

waypoints.event: wandered in the wilderness of Beer-sheba
Ishmael settled in the wilderness of Paran

journeys.description: Abraham's journey from Beer-sheba to Mount Moriah for the binding of Isaac, and return

waypoints.event: departed from Beer-sheba (inferred starting point)
went to the land of Moriah to offer Isaac
returned together to Beer-sheba and dwelt there

journeys.description: Abraham's servant travels from Canaan to Aram-Naharaim to find a wife for Isaac, and returns

waypoints.event: departed from Abraham's household (Beer-sheba area)
traveled to Aram-Naharaim, city of Nahor
returned; Isaac came from Beer-lahai-roi to meet Rebekah

journeys.description: Isaac sojourns in Gerar during famine, digs wells, and settles at Beer-sheba

waypoints.event: went to Abimelech king of the Philistines in Gerar
moved to the valley of Gerar, dug wells
dug a well; herdsmen quarreled, named it Esek
dug another well; quarreled again, named it Sitnah
dug a third well with no quarrel; named it Rehoboth
went up to Beer-sheba
named the well Shibah; hence Beer-sheba

journeys.description: Esau moves from Canaan to the hill country of Seir (Edom)

waypoints.event: took all his household and possessions acquired in Canaan
settled in the hill country of Seir (Edom)

journeys.description: Jacob flees from Esau, traveling from Beer-sheba via Bethel to Haran (Paddan-Aram)

waypoints.event: left Beer-sheba
slept at Luz; dreamed of the ladder; named it Bethel
arrived at Haran, met Rachel at the well

journeys.description: Jacob's return from Haran through Gilead, across the Jabbok, and to Shechem in Canaan

waypoints.event: departed from Paddan-Aram with all his possessions
crossed the Euphrates and headed toward the hill country of Gilead
made covenant with Laban at Mizpah (Galeed)
met angels of God; named the place Mahanaim
crossed the ford of the Jabbok with his family
wrestled with God; named the place Peniel
built a house and made booths; named the place Succoth
arrived safely at the city of Shechem in Canaan

journeys.description: Jacob travels from Shechem to Bethel, then to Ephrath (Bethlehem), Migdal-Eder, and finally Hebron

waypoints.event: buried foreign gods under the oak near Shechem before departing
came to Luz (Bethel); built an altar called El Bethel
journeyed from Bethel; Rachel died on the way to Ephrath (Bethlehem)
pitched tent beyond Migdal-Eder (tower of the flock)
came to Isaac at Mamre (Kiriath-arba), that is Hebron

journeys.description: Joseph is sent from Hebron to check on his brothers, finds them at Dothan, is sold and taken to Egypt

waypoints.event: sent from the valley of Hebron by Jacob
came to Shechem looking for his brothers
found his brothers at Dothan
sold to Ishmaelite/Midianite traders who brought him to Egypt

journeys.description: Judah separates from his brothers, goes to Adullam, and later travels to Timnah for sheep-shearing

waypoints.event: went down from his brothers and turned aside to an Adullamite
went up to Timnah for sheep-shearing
Tamar sat at the entrance to Enaim on the road to Timnah

journeys.description: Joseph's ten brothers travel from Canaan to Egypt to buy grain during the famine, and return

waypoints.event: departed from famine-stricken Canaan
went down to Egypt to buy grain
returned to Jacob in the land of Canaan

journeys.description: The brothers return to Egypt with Benjamin, are received by Joseph, and return to Canaan

waypoints.event: grain from Egypt consumed; must return
went down to Egypt with Benjamin and stood before Joseph
went up out of Egypt and came to Canaan with news of Joseph

journeys.description: Jacob and his entire household migrate from Canaan to Egypt, stopping at Beer-sheba and settling in Goshen

waypoints.event: took livestock and possessions acquired in Canaan
stopped at Beer-sheba and offered sacrifices to God
came to Egypt — Jacob and all his offspring
sent Judah ahead to direct the way to Goshen
Joseph met Jacob in Goshen; settled in the land of Goshen
Joseph settled his family in the land of Rameses

journeys.description: Jacob's funeral procession from Egypt to the cave of Machpelah at Hebron, and return to Egypt

waypoints.event: departed Egypt with elders, servants, and chariots
mourned at the threshing floor of Atad beyond the Jordan (Abel-Mizraim)
buried Jacob in the cave of Machpelah before Mamre (Hebron)
Joseph and all who went with him returned to Egypt
